description: Abilities inspiring and commanding others through force of personality — rallying allies, tactical coordination, and the power of story and song.
abilities: Attendant

Attracting a capable apprentice from the surrounding population who serves as a permanent companion until dismissed. Only one attendant may be retained at a time. The attendant is a martial ally with respectable fighting ability, capable of counterattacking, unleashing wild strikes, and provoking enemies. They run errands, assist in combat, and follow orders, but may refuse commands that are suicidal, absurd, or morally unconscionable. The arrangement is built on mutual respect, and betrayal may drive them away.
Bond

Forging an unbreakable partnership with a chosen ally who willingly accepts the bond. Only one such bond may exist at a time, lasting until the partner dies or departs. The bonded fighter can intercept attacks aimed at their partner without hesitation, senses when they are in danger even across great distances, becomes immune to fear when their partner is near death, and feels their own heart beat stronger in their partner's presence.
Champion

Having earned legendary renown through heroic deeds that have spread across the land. Those who value power defer in reverence, and authoritarian-minded figures such as guards and bandits are especially susceptible to the fighter's prestige. This fame grants the ability to recruit admirers effortlessly and to attract a permanent martial attendant.
Commune

Speaking the language of outland folk to gain the trust of a nearby common person, exchanging a local saying -- something like "It's raining cats and dogs" or "Don't judge a book by its cover" -- and explaining its meaning. The gesture of kinship opens the person to answering questions about who is causing trouble, where the local leader can be found, and what people have been talking about lately.
Folk Song

Singing a song that kindles strong feelings in non-hostile listeners nearby. The caster performs a chorus -- original or borrowed -- and chooses a mood to set: bright, swelling hearts with friendly enthusiasm and sparking joyful conversation; somber, drawing the audience into sorrowful silence and reflection on their suffering; or proud, igniting zealous feelings that make the crowd noisy and excitable.
Inspire

Reciting a meaningful statement, a famous proverb, or a phrase of personal invention, to a nearby creature that can hear and understand the words and is not currently hostile. Until the end of the day, the inspired creature shapes its behavior around its highest ideal and cannot fall victim to its worst flaw.
Marshal

Organizing nearby allies to overcome a serious test of collective strength. The entire group throws their effort into the challenge, and if the majority succeed, even improbable feats become possible: breaking through reinforced barriers, lifting crushing weight, or overpowering a vastly stronger opponent.
Plan

Seizing the advantage when time permits preparation before a conflict. The fighter orchestrates an ambush in which every ally declares their opening action, and all strike simultaneously before the enemy can react.
Recruit

Commanding a non-hostile common person or lesser foe to lend their service. The recruit may join a fight, guard a position, deliver a message, or perform other practical tasks to the best of their ability for up to a day. They will not follow absurd or suicidal orders.
Speak Myth

Appealing to local mythology to win the favor of a common person, weaving a tale from four parts: an obligation the listener will feel compelled to honor -- offering shelter and secrecy, granting a fair trade, running an errand, confessing a wrongdoing, forgiving a transgression, spreading a rumor, or some other harmless request; a lesson the myth teaches, such as "Give refuge to strangers" or "Always be a fair dealer"; a central person or event the myth revolves around; and a dramatic story describing what that person or event is famous for. The myth, once told, settles into the listener's mind like an old truth.
Summon The Blood

Bolstering the spirits of companions during a rest by reciting a stirring poem, lending the words a healing resonance that mends weary bodies and lifts flagging morale beyond what rest alone can provide.
Valiant Soliloquy

Delivering a rousing speech that ignites courage in every ally who hears it. Those inspired gain a second chance on their next faltering effort, though the inspiration fades if not seized before the moment passes.
War Story

Recounting a battle from past adventures during a period of calm, drawing renewed energy and resolve from the retelling. A different conflict must be recalled each time, whether drawn from true experience or the fighter's storied past.
